SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.dashboard

Class DashboardAutoConfiguration

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.dashboard.DashboardAutoConfiguration
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer
@AutoConfiguration(afterName={"org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.MetricsAutoConfiguration","org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.CompositeMeterRegistryAutoConfiguration","org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.export.simple.SimpleMetricsExportAutoConfiguration"}) @ConditionalOnWebApplication(type=SERVLET) @ConditionalOnClass(io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry.class) @ConditionalOnProperty(prefix="failover.dashboard", name="enabled", havingValue="true") @EnableConfigurationProperties(DashboardProperties.class) public class DashboardAutoConfiguration extends Object implements org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer
Auto-configuration for the embedded failover dashboard.

Secure-by-default (design doc §1a): an absent failover.dashboard.enabled property means off — no matchIfMissing. The consumer must explicitly opt in via YAML. With the property unset, no bean, controller, resource handler, JSON, or UI is mapped at all.

All dashboard surfaces share the single DashboardProperties.basePath() namespace: the static UI is served from basePath/** and the JSON API is mapped under basePath/api. The validated base path is used verbatim by both, so they cannot collide with consumer services or drift apart.

P0/P1 scope: master switch, properties, static-asset resource handler, and the config API/view. Metrics services and UI arrive in P2–P3.

Author:
Anand Manissery

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addInterceptors(org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.InterceptorRegistry registry)
    Enforces exposure.include narrowing and the CSP header on every base-path/** request.
    void
    addResourceHandlers(org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.ResourceHandlerRegistry registry)
     
    void
    addViewControllers(org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.ViewControllerRegistry registry)
    Welcome mapping: a bare base-path (and its trailing-slash form) forwards to the static index.html, so /failover-dashboard opens the UI without the explicit file name.

      @Bean @ConditionalOnMissingBean public DashboardController dashboardController(DashboardConfigService configService)

    • dashboardMetricsService

      @Bean @ConditionalOnBean(io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry.class) @ConditionalOnMissingBean public DashboardMetricsService dashboardMetricsService(io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry registry)
      Metrics service + controller — present only when a MeterRegistry is in the context. Without Micrometer the config view above still works; the metrics view degrades gracefully (§3).

    • dashboardMetricsController

      @Bean @ConditionalOnBean(DashboardMetricsService.class) @ConditionalOnMissingBean public DashboardMetricsController dashboardMetricsController(DashboardMetricsService metricsService)

    • addInterceptors

      public void addInterceptors(org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.InterceptorRegistry registry)
      Enforces exposure.include narrowing and the CSP header on every base-path/** request.
      Specified by:
      addInterceptors in interface org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer

    • addResourceHandlers

      public void addResourceHandlers(org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.ResourceHandlerRegistry registry)
      Specified by:
      addResourceHandlers in interface org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer

    • addViewControllers

      public void addViewControllers(org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.ViewControllerRegistry registry)
      Welcome mapping: a bare base-path (and its trailing-slash form) forwards to the static index.html, so /failover-dashboard opens the UI without the explicit file name. A forward (not redirect) keeps the URL clean and lets the resource handler serve the page. Skipped when UI exposure is narrowed off.
      Specified by:
      addViewControllers in interface org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer
